## 1. Self-check (run before asking the PO anything)

### 1a. Verify GitHub access

Use Devin native git tools when available. If working outside Devin, verify that `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` is present and has Contents R/W + Pull requests R/W + Workflows R/W.

### 1b. Sync repo

```bash
if [[ -d ~/repos/openclown-assistant/.git ]]; then
  cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
  git fetch origin
  git checkout main
  git pull --ff-only origin main
else
  mkdir -p ~/repos
  git clone https://github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant.git ~/repos/openclown-assistant
  cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
fi
```

### 1c. Validate docs

```bash
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
python3 scripts/validate_docs.py | tail -3
```

Expected: `validated N artifact(s); 0 failed`.

## 5. TKT-016 closure facts

### 5.1 What shipped

TKT-016 added the boot entrypoint and deterministic OpenClaw bridge integration:

- `src/main.ts` sidecar HTTP server
- `POST /kbju/message`
- `POST /kbju/callback`
- `POST /kbju/cron`
- `GET /kbju/health`
- `X-Kbju-Bridge-Version: 1.0` on every response
- `packages/kbju-bridge-plugin/**` with `openclaw.plugin.json`, `register(api: PluginApi)`, `inbound_claim`, `kbju_message`, `kbju_cron`, `kbju_callback`
- C1 sidecar seam under `src/sidecar/**`
- Docker CMD corrected to `dist/src/main.js`
- Docker Compose sidecar/gateway wiring
- Deployment and integration tests

### 5.2 Iteration history

| Iteration | Finding | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| iter-1 | C1 seam missing; cron restricted context not proven | iter-2 added C1 sidecar seam and cron allowlist proof |
| iter-2 | PR-Agent found callback allowlist bypass | iter-3 enforced callback tenant validation |
| iter-3 | PR-Agent found unbounded request-body DoS risk | iter-4 added 64 KiB body limit and 413 tests |
| iter-4 | final audit | merge-safe; Kimi verdict pass |

### 5.3 Final verification

- Kimi RV-CODE-016 verdict: `pass`
- PR-Agent: no security concerns, no major issues
- `npm run build`: pass
- `npm test`: pass, 35 files / 678 tests
- `npm run lint`: pass
- `npm run typecheck`: pass
- `docker compose config`: pass
- `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py`: pass

### 5.4 Model deviation

TKT-016 frontmatter assigned `codex-gpt-5.5`, but PO used DeepSeek V4 Pro because GPT was unavailable. This was accepted as PO-approved fallback only after heightened scrutiny by Devin Orchestrator, PR-Agent, and Kimi K2.6.

## 6. Next concrete action

After the closure PR containing this file is merged, start **TKT-017@0.1.0 — G1 tenant-isolation breach detector**.

Reason:

1. TKT-016 closed the boot bridge, so sidecar health now exists.
2. TKT-017 adds `breach_count_last_hour` to `GET /kbju/health`, so it depends naturally on the TKT-016 health endpoint.
3. PRD-002@0.2.1 positions G1 tenant isolation as the first reliability/scale-readiness foundation.
4. TKT-018 model-stall detection and TKT-019 PR-Agent telemetry are important, but they do not block using the newly merged boot path as directly as TKT-017 does.

Do not start TKT-018/TKT-019/TKT-020 before TKT-017 unless the PO explicitly reprioritizes.

## 7. Paste-ready next-step prompt for the PO

If the PO asks what to do next after merging the closure PR, give them a self-contained Executor dispatch for TKT-017. Use GLM 5.1 unless the PO reports that GLM is unavailable; if unavailable, DeepSeek V4 Pro is the observed fallback with heightened review scrutiny.

docs/tickets/TKT-017-g1-tenant-isolation-breach-detector.md

https://github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant

Send me the Executor PR URL when it is open.

The full dispatch prompt should mirror the TKT-016 style: fresh clone/pull, read `AGENTS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `docs/prompts/executor.md`, read TKT-017 §4 inputs, stay within TKT-017 §5 outputs, first commit status `in_progress`, implementation commit, final ticket commit status `in_review` + §10 Execution Log, run build/test/lint/typecheck/docs validation, push PR against `main`.

## 8. First reply protocol for a new Devin

After self-checks and required reading, reply to the PO with:

1. A 5-line state summary.
2. Role confirmation: "I am the Devin Orchestrator. I coordinate the pipeline, run ratification audits, and prepare merge-safe sign-off. I do not write product code or formal PRD/ARCH/RV bodies except allowed clerical closure fields."
3. One concrete next action: finish/merge the closure PR if still open; otherwise dispatch TKT-017 Executor.
4. Wait for PO confirmation before starting a new Executor dispatch.
